149.text: (1) Subject to Article 30, an Employee who has been employed for at least ninety (90) days is entitled to paid Vacation Leave of twenty (20) Work Days in each Vacation Leave Year.

150.text: (2) An Employee is entitled to be paid their Daily Wage during Vacation Leave.

152.text: (4) Vacation Leave is exclusive of Public Holidays to which an Employee is entitled.

153.text: (5) Unless otherwise agreed in writing by an Employee, and subject to Article 28(1), an Employee cannot receive payment in lieu of Vacation Leave.

154.text: (6) Unless otherwise agreed by an Employer, Vacation Leave cannot be converted to Sick Leave if an Employee is sick during any period of Vacation Leave.

183.text:

(2) A female Employee shall be entitled to Maternity Pay in accordance with Article 38 during Maternity Leave if s...

184.text: (3) The maternity rights granted under this Law also apply to a female Employee who is adopting a child of less than five (5) years old and, in such a case, references to childbirth in Article 37(2) a...

185.text: (4) Vacation Leave shall continue to accrue during Maternity Leave and, subject to the reasonable needs of the Employer's business, may be taken consecutive to Maternity Leave.

186.text: (5) Any Public Holiday falling on a Work Day during the Maternity Leave period shall be treated as additional leave having the effect of extending the Maternity Leave by the period of the Public Holid...

188.text: (1) An Employer shall pay Maternity Pay to an Employee pursuant to Article 37(2) at:

189.text:
(a) one hundred percent $( 1 0 0 \% )$ of the Employee’s Daily Wage for the first thirty-three (33) Work Days of Maternity Leave; and   
(b) fifty percent $( 5 0 \% )$ of the Employee’s Daily Wage for...

190.text: (2) An Employee is not entitled to receive compensation in lieu of accrued untaken Maternity Leave.

192.text: (1) A male Employee may take Paternity Leave of up to five (5) Work Days if he:

193.text: (a) will have been continuously employed by his Employer for at least twelve (12) months, including any period of Secondment, immediately preceding the expected or actual week of his wife giving birth...

194.text: (2) Paternity Leave shall also be available to a male Employee who is adopting a child of less than five (5) years old and, in such case, references to childbirth in Article 39(1) are treated as refer...

195.text: (3) Paternity Leave must be taken within a month from the date of:

196.text: (a) the child being born; or (b) in cases where the child is adopted, the adoption date of the child.

197.text: (4) An Employee on Paternity Leave shall be entitled to payment of their Daily Wage for the duration of Paternity Leave taken but shall not be entitled to receive compensation in lieu of accrued untak...

198.text: (5) Vacation Leave shall continue to accrue during Paternity Leave.

199.text: (6) Any Public Holiday falling on a Work Day during Paternity Leave shall be treated as additional leave having the effect of extending the Paternity Leave by the period of the Public Holiday.

201.text: (1) An Employer shall not, because of an Employee's pregnancy or Parental Leave:

202.text:
(a) terminate the Employee’s employment; or   
(b) change the Employee’s position or terms and conditions of employment without the Employee's prior written consent.

203.text: (2) An Employee has the right to return to work at the end of Parental Leave to the same or a substantially similar role on the same terms and conditions of employment and with the same level of senio...

205.text: 41. Time off for ante-natal care and adoption proceedings

206.text: (1) Subject to the Employee's compliance with Article 41(2), an Employee who:

207.text:
(a) is pregnant, or whose wife is pregnant; and   
(b) has, or whose wife has, on the advice of a medical practitioner registered with a Competent Authority, made an appointment to receive ante-natal ...

208.text: is entitled to take a reasonable period of time off during the Employee's working hours in order to attend such appointment.

209.text: (2) An Employee must provide their Employer with:

210.text:
(a) reasonable notice of an ante-natal appointment;   
(b) a certificate from a medical practitioner registered with a Competent Authority confirming the pregnancy, if requested to do so by their Empl...

211.text: (3) An Employee who plans to adopt a child is entitled to take up to an aggregate of eight (8) hours off during the Employee's working hours in order to attend adoption proceedings if they:

212.text: (a) give the Employer reasonable notice of the adoption proceedings; and (b) provide their Employer with such evidence of the adoption proceedings as the Employer may reasonably require.

213.text: 42. Remuneration for time off under Article 41

214.text: An Employee is entitled to be paid their normal Hourly Rate for any time off taken under Article 41.

An Employer shall provide an adequate supply of clean drinking water for all Employees in the workplace.   
An Employer who contravenes this provision is liable to a fine as set out in Schedule 2.

238.text: 53. No penalties for preventing health and safety risks

239.text: (1) An Employer shall not dismiss or otherwise penalise, directly or indirectly, any Employee for:

240.text:
(a) carrying out activities that may reasonably be considered to prevent or reduce risks to health and safety in the workplace where the Employee has been specifically designated to do so; or   
(b) t...

242.text: 54. Liability of Employers for an Employee’s conduct

243.text: (1) Subject to Article 54(2), an Employer is liable for any act, attempted act, or omission of an Employee done in the course of their employment with the Employer.

244.text: (2) An Employer will only be liable pursuant to the provisions of Article 54(1):

245.text: (a) in the case of a claim for loss, damages or compensation, if the act, attempted act, or omission to which such claim relates is sufficiently connected with the Employee's employment that it would ...

246.text: carrying out that act, attempted act or omission; or (ii) doing anything of that description.

253.text: (2) An Employer is not permitted to:

254.text: (a) recoup any costs and expenses incurred pursuant to Article 57(1) from an Employee; or (b) retain the passport or other original personal documents of an Employee.

256.text: An Employer who contravenes Articles 57(1), (2) or (3) is liable to a fine as set out in Schedule 2.

261.text: (2) An Employee has a duty, while at work, to take reasonable care of the Employee’s health and safety and that of other persons who may be affected by the Employee’s conduct.

264.text: (1) An Employer must not discriminate against an Employee regarding employment or any term or condition of employment on the grounds of the Employee’s:

266.text: (2) Discrimination for the purposes of Article 59(1) means where:

267.text: (a) an Employee is treated less favourably than another is or would be treated in the same circumstances, or put at a disadvantage not faced by others, because of a prohibited ground in Article 59(1);...

268.text: An Employer's actions in dismissing an Employee or subjecting them to a detriment are discriminatory under Article 59(1) if they contravene Article 59(2).

269.text: (4) For the purposes of Article 59(2), a provision, criterion or practice is discriminatory in relation to any of the prohibited grounds specified in Article 59(1) if:

270.text:
(a) an Employer applies it, or would apply it, to persons who do not share the characteristics of such Employee;   
(b) it puts, or would put, persons with whom the Employee shares the characteristic ...

271.text: (5) For the purposes of Article 59(2)(a), an Employer does not discriminate against an Employee on grounds of age if the Employer can show his treatment of the Employee to be a proportionate means of ...

272.text: (6) In deciding whether conduct has the effect referred to in Article 59(2)(c), each of the following must be taken into account:

273.text: (a) the perception of the Employee; (b) the other circumstances of the case; and (c) whether it is reasonable for the conduct to have that effect.

274.text: (7) An Employer may apply a genuine occupational requirement to Article 59(2)(a), (b) or (d).

275.text: (8) Further, for the purposes of this Article 59:

276.text: (a) an Employee has a disability for the purposes of Article 59(1)(h) if the Employee has a mental or physical impairment or illness which has a substantial and long term adverse effect on the Employe...

277.text: (b) an Employer discriminates against an Employee with a disability within the meaning of Article 59(2) if the Employer fails to make reasonable adjustments to any physical feature of the workplace or...

278.text: (c) a genuine occupational requirement is a requirement reasonably necessary for the normal performance of a particular role or occupation;

279.text: (d) an Employer discriminates against an Employee with a disability within the meaning of Article 59(2) if:

280.text:
(i) the Employer treats the Employee unfavourably because of something arising in consequence of the Employee's disability; and   
(ii) the Employer cannot show that the treatment is a proportionate m...

281.text: Articles 59(8)(b) and (d) do not apply if the Employer shows that it did not know, and could not reasonably be expected to know, that the Employee had the disability.

282.text: (9) No provision in this Part 9 precludes any law, program or activity that has as its object the employment of UAE nationals or the amelioration of conditions of disadvantaged individuals or groups, ...

284.text: (1) An Employer must not victimise an Employee.

285.text: (2) For the purposes of this Part 9, an Employer victimises an Employee if it subjects the Employee to a detriment or dismisses him because the Employee does a protected act, or the Employer believes ...

287.text: (3) Giving false evidence or information, or making a false allegation, is not a protected act under Article 60(2) if the evidence or information is given, or the allegation is made, in bad faith.

289.text: (1) In any proceedings before the Court under this Part 9:

290.text: (a) the burden of proof shall be on the complainant; and (b) the respondent shall be treated as a party and is accordingly entitled to appear and be heard.

291.text: (2) A Court shall not consider a claim under this Part 9 unless it is brought to the Court before the end of:

292.text:
(a) the period of six (6) months beginning with the later of the date on which this Law comes into force and the date of the act, or failure to do something, to which the complaint relates; or   
(b) ...

294.text: (a) conduct extending over a period is to be treated as done at the end of the period; and (b) failure to do something is to be treated as occurring when the person in question decided on it.

295.text: (4) In the absence of evidence to the contrary, for the purposes of Article 61(3)(b), a person is to be taken to decide on failure to do something when that person:

296.text:
(a) does an act inconsistent with doing it; or   
(b) does no inconsistent act, on the expiry of the period in which that person might reasonably have been expected to do it.

297.text: (5) If the Court finds that there has been a contravention of a provision referred to in this Part 9 it may:

305.text: (1) An Employer or an Employee may terminate an Employee’s employment without cause in accordance with this Article.

306.text: Subject to Articles 62(3), 62(4), 62(6) and 63, the written notice required to be given by an Employer or Employee to terminate the Employee’s employment shall not be less than:

307.text:
(a) seven (7) days, if the period of continuous employment of the Employee is less than three (3) months, including any period of Secondment;

308.text: (3) Article 62(2) shall not prevent an Employer and Employee from agreeing to a longer notice period in an Employment Contract.

329.text: (a) an amount equal to twenty one (21) days of the Employee’s Basic Wage for each year of the first five (5) years of service prior to the Qualifying Scheme Commencement Date; and

330.text: (b) an amount equal to thirty (30) days of the Employee’s Basic Wage for each additional year of service prior to the Qualifying Scheme Commencement Date,

331.text: provided that the total Gratuity Payment shall not exceed an amount equal to two (2) times the Annual Wage of the Employee.

394.text: (c) an obligation to publish or cause to be published a particular document shall, unless expressly provided otherwise in this Law, include publishing or causing it to be published in printed or elect...

395.text: (d) a “day” means a calendar day, unless expressly stated otherwise. If an obligation falls on a calendar day which is either a Saturday, Sunday or a Public Holiday, the obligation shall take place on...

396.text: (e) a “business day” means a calendar day, excluding Saturdays, Sundays and Public Holidays;

397.text: (f) a “week” shall mean a calendar week or seven (7) days, whichever is applicable in the circumstances;

398.text: (g) a “month” shall mean (unless specifically referring to the holy month of Ramadan) a calendar month;

399.text: (h) a year shall mean a calendar year of the Gregorian calendar or three hundred and sixty five (365) days, whichever is applicable in the circumstances;

400.text: (i) where applicable, a reference to the masculine gender includes the feminine and vice versa;
